3.speaker: Crab
3.text: I do not know what that is.

choices.text: Way to talk to anyone in the world.

4_1_1.speaker: Crab
4_1_1.text: Learn? And what kinds of things can you learn?

4_1_3.speaker: Crab
4_1_3.text: What does this 'internet' say about me?

4_1_4.speaker: You
4_1_4.text: Let me search you up and see...

4_1_7.speaker: You
4_1_7.text: Turns out you aren't even a crab or crustacean. You're closer to a spider.

4_1_8.speaker: Crab
4_1_8.text: That is... both unsettling and very insightful.

4_1_10.speaker: You
4_1_10.text: Your species only has a lifespan of around twenty years.

4_1_11.speaker: You
4_1_11.text: If that's the case, then how do you know all about the past?

4_1_12.speaker: Crab
4_1_12.text: It was my mother who taught me. And her mother told her. And all before. Perhaps it's in our DNA.

4_1_13.speaker: You
4_1_13.text: Oh, so you've been told these stories before?

4_1_14.speaker: Crab
4_1_14.text: Not stories, simply memories with no meaning.

4_2_2.speaker: You
4_2_2.text: Instantly. No matter where they are.

4_2_3.speaker: Crab
4_2_3.text: And what do you say to them? All these people, everywhere at once?

4_2_4.speaker: You
4_2_4.text: Everything. News, opinions, memories... Sometimes nothing important at all.

4_2_5.speaker: Crab
4_2_5.text: So you built something that could reach every corner of the world... and you use it to tell stories.

4_2_6.speaker: You
4_2_6.text: I never thought of it like that. But yes, I guess so.

4_2_7.speaker: Crab
4_2_7.text: That has not changed at all.

5.speaker: Crab
5.text: As I look around here, I see so much innovation. You humans have changed so much over the years.

7.speaker: Crab
7.text: Look at all of this. None of this existed when my ancestors first crawled ashore.

8.speaker: You
8.text: The tools changed, yes. But us people? We do it all because we are human. And part of humanity is to build something new.

10.speaker: You
10.text: You don't need some big transformation, some evolution to change.

11.speaker: Crab
11.text: I had not thought of it that way.

12.speaker: You
12.text: Maybe that's the point. Maybe changing your perspective is enough to change who you are.

13.speaker: Crab
13.text: A new perspective... is its own kind of evolution?

14.speaker: Crab
14.text: I... I think I feel slightly different.

16.speaker: Crab
16.text: I think I am ready to move on.
